A dog in Washington State, US, has survived being hit by a car, an attempted mercy killing and a burial. The black and white bully breed mix, named Theia, was found emaciated and covered in dirt four days after she was buried. She was taken in by resident Sara Mellado who said: "She's a true miracle dog and she deserves a good life". Theia, who is just a year old, suffered a dislocated jaw, leg injuries and a caved-in sinus cavity. A fundraising page set up to help pay for her medical care has already surpassed its $10,000 target.

text: A stray dog in Washington State has miraculously survived after being hit by a car, hit on the head with a hammer, and buried in a field. The dog, now named Theia, managed to stagger to a nearby farm four days after her apparent death, where she was found by a worker and taken to a vet for help. Theia suffered from a dislocated jaw, leg injuries, and a caved-in sinus cavity. She still requires surgery to help her breathe. The community has rallied together and exceeded the $10,000 fundraising target set to cover the dog's medical expenses. text: As the world watches the development of the Iran nuclear deal, the Iranian Foreign Minister Mohammad Javad Zarif has become a crucial figure. Zarif has been Secretary of State John Kerry's opposite number in negotiating a nuclear deal that could lead to an end to sanctions on Iran. Zarif has developed a reputation for being 'polished' and 'jovial', and has been hailed as the man who could bring Iran back into the international community. However, there are less well-known aspects to Zarif's career, including his tweet to mark the Jewish New Year in 2013, a move which triggered a discussion about Iran's Holocaust denial. Zarif was also nominated as Foreign Minister by the successor to the notorious former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad. Zarif's own background includes a period of study in the US, where he earned an undergraduate degree from San Francisco State University and a doctorate from the University of Denver. Zarif is also reportedly under investigation by US authorities over his alleged role in controlling a charitable organization accused of money laundering.

text: Mohammad Javad Zarif, the Iranian foreign minister, has been instrumental in securing a breakthrough in nuclear discussions with the US that could lead to an end to sanctions against Iran. Zarif received a hero's welcome upon his return to Iran and is seen as a key figure in bringing Iran back into the international community. However, there are some lesser-known facts about Zarif. In 2013, he tweeted "Happy Rosh Hashanah" to mark the Jewish New Year, prompting a response from Christine Pelosi, daughter of House Minority Leader Nancy Pelosi, asking him to end Iran's Holocaust denial. Zarif replied that Iran never denied the Holocaust and referred to the former Iranian President, Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, who had left office the previous month. Zarif was nominated as foreign minister by Ahmadinejad's successor, Hassan Rouhami. Zarif left Iran in 1977 and studied in the US, receiving his undergraduate degree from San Francisco State University and his master's and doctorate from the University of Denver. He has been investigated by the US Justice Department in relation to the Alavi Foundation, but his name was not mentioned in the settlement. Zarif has spent more time with US Secretary of State John Kerry than any other foreign minister, as they work towards finalizing the nuclear agreement by June 30.
